ThreadOutcome.description: Terminal outcome of one work-list thread (see CONTEXT.md — "Thread outcome").
ThreadOutcome.title: ThreadOutcome

description:
One resolution turn's validated output: the verdict on a single review thread.

The agent judges the thread (worth? safe?), implements + signed-commits when warranted, and
returns this. GitHub side effects (reply, resolve) are performed by the driver from this
verdict — the agent never posts comments or resolves threads itself.

thread_id.description: The review thread's node id, echoed back verbatim from the turn's input.
thread_id.title: Thread Id

outcome.description: fixed: implemented and committed via the signed-commit tooling. wont_fix: deliberately declined (not worth doing). already_fixed: the current code already addresses it. obsolete: superseded by other changes (name them in the reply). escalate: worth doing but not safe to do unattended — a human must decide.

reasoning.description: Internal assessment for the work log: the worth/safe judgment and what was checked against the current code, with file:line evidence.
reasoning.title: Reasoning

reply.description: The reply to post on the thread: plain, self-contained language a reader can act on without opening the code — what was done (or why not) and what happens next.
reply.title: Reply

commit_sha.description: The pushed commit's SHA exactly as reported by the signed-commit tooling. Required when outcome is fixed; null otherwise.
commit_sha.title: Commit Sha

verification.description: What was run to verify a fix (lint, tests) and the honest result, failures included.
verification.title: Verification

title: ThreadResolution
